title: TC-DFU-LEG50-001: Verify 'can't be updated' message displayed for Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case on outdated firmware
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset is connected to the phone via Bluetooth. The charge case has outdated firmware (e.g., version 1.0.0 where 1.2.0 is available).
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name on the home screen.
3. Navigate to Device Settings → Software.
4. Observe the Software screen for the charge case component.
5. Verify the charge case firmware version is displayed as outdated (e.g., '1.0.0').
6. Verify the text 'can't be updated' message is displayed adjacent to or below the charge case firmware version.
7. Verify there is NO update button, 'Update' text button, or any actionable UI element next to the charge case component.
8. Verify the headset component (if shown) still displays its own firmware version and any applicable update button (if outdated).
expected: The Software screen displays the charge case firmware version with a 'can't be updated' message and no update button. The headset component's UI remains unaffected.

title: TC-DFU-LEG50-002: Verify 'can't be updated' message persists after app restart for Legend 50 Charge Case
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset is connected. Charge case has outdated firmware.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
3. Confirm the charge case shows 'can't be updated' message with no update button.
4. Press the Home button to background the app.
5. Swipe the app away from the recent apps list to force-kill Poly Studio Mobile.
6. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
8. Observe the charge case component on the Software screen.
expected: After app restart, the charge case still displays the 'can't be updated' message with no update button. The error state persists across app lifecycle events.

title: TC-DFU-LEG50-003: Verify 'can't be updated' message persists after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect for Legend 50 Charge Case
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset is connected. Charge case has outdated firmware.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
3. Confirm the charge case shows 'can't be updated' message with no update button.
4. Press the Bluetooth button on the Voyager Legend 50 headset for 2 seconds to disconnect.
5. Wait 5 seconds.
6. Press the Bluetooth button on the Voyager Legend 50 headset for 2 seconds to reconnect.
7. Wait for the device to appear as connected in Poly Studio Mobile (check home screen).
8. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
9. Observe the charge case component on the Software screen.
expected: After Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect, the charge case still displays the 'can't be updated' message with no update button. The error state persists across Bluetooth state transitions.

title: TC-DFU-LEG50-004: Verify headset update button remains functional when charge case shows 'can't be updated' on Legend 50
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset is connected. Both headset AND charge case have outdated firmware.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
3. Verify the charge case component shows 'can't be updated' message with no update button.
4. Verify the headset component shows its outdated firmware version (e.g., '2.0.0' where '2.2.0' is available).
5. Verify an 'Update' button is present next to the headset component.
6. Tap the 'Update' button for the headset.
7. Observe the behavior.
expected: The headset update initiates successfully despite the charge case showing 'can't be updated'. The charge case error state does not block the headset firmware update flow.

title: TC-DFU-LEG50-005: Verify Software screen UI layout when charge case shows 'can't be updated' on Legend 50
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset is connected. Charge case has outdated firmware.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
3. Observe the full Software screen layout.
4. Verify the screen title reads 'Software' or 'Firmware Update'.

5. Verify the charge case component is displayed with: a) Component name (e.g., 'Charge Case'), b) Current firmware version number, c) 'can't be updated' message in readable text, d) No update button, icon, or tap target.
6. Verify the headset component (if shown) is displayed with its own firmware version and any applicable update UI.
7. Verify no error popup, dialog, or crash occurs when viewing the screen.
8. Verify the back button or navigation bar is functional and returns to the previous screen.
expected: The Software screen renders correctly with all UI elements visible. The charge case shows the 'can't be updated' message cleanly without any popup errors or crashes. Navigation remains functional.

title: TC-DFU-LEG50-006: Verify 'can't be updated' message persists after phone reboot for Legend 50 Charge Case
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset is connected. Charge case has outdated firmware.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
3. Confirm the charge case shows 'can't be updated' message with no update button.
4. Close Poly Studio Mobile app.
5. Reboot the phone.
6. After phone restarts, enable Bluetooth.
7. Wait for Voyager Legend 50 to reconnect automatically (or manually pair if needed).
8. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
9. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
10. Observe the charge case component on the Software screen.
expected: After phone reboot, the charge case still displays the 'can't be updated' message with no update button. The error state persists across phone reboot.

title: TC-DFU-LEG50-007: Verify charge case firmware version is correctly reported when 'can't be updated' is shown on Legend 50
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset is connected. Charge case has outdated firmware version 1.0.0 (latest is 1.2.0).
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
3. Locate the charge case component on the Software screen.
4. Note the firmware version number displayed for the charge case.
5. Verify the displayed version number matches the actual charge case firmware (e.g., '1.0.0').
6. Verify the 'can't be updated' message is displayed.
7. Verify no 'Up to date' or 'Update available' text is shown — only the error message.
expected: The charge case firmware version is accurately reported. The 'can't be updated' message correctly indicates the error state rather than falsely showing 'Up to date' or providing an update button.

title: TC-DFU-LEG50-008: Verify no partial update UI elements appear for Legend 50 Charge Case in 'can't be updated' state
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset is connected. Charge case has outdated firmware.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the connected Voyager Legend 50 device name → Device Settings → Software.
3. Observe the charge case component area carefully.
4. Verify there is no grayed-out or disabled 'Update' button.
5. Verify there is no 'Update' text that is non-tappable.
6. Verify there is no progress bar, spinner, or loading indicator.
7. Verify there is no 'Retry' button or link.
8. Verify there is no 'Check for updates' button next to the charge case.
9. Verify the only UI element related to the charge case update state is the firmware version number and the 'can't be updated' text message.
expected: No partial, disabled, or non-functional update UI elements are present. The charge case component cleanly shows only the version number and the 'can't be updated' message without misleading the user with non-functional controls.
